data.content:
Build a simple Soirée Planner web app in this project.

The app manages a three-night summer soirée. Add a guest list showing
each guest's name, RSVP status, and plus-one. Add a signature drink
menu with one drink per night. Add a countdown to golden hour on
opening night. Style it like a summer evening party: warm dusk colors,
string-light energy. Keep the code simple and readable.
I’m setting up a compact web app in the workspace and will verify it runs locally once the initial implementation is in place.
The layout and content are being assembled as a polished one-page experience with dynamic countdown behavior and simple data-driven sections.

toolRequests.name: create_file
toolRequests.arguments:

A warm, lantern-lit weekend of music, citrus cocktails, and late-night laughter.

RSVPs are glowing up.

Signature drinks

One candlelit sip for each evening.

"}

create_file

data.content: The page structure and styling are in place; I’m running a quick local check now to confirm the app serves correctly.

toolRequests.name: run_in_terminal
toolRequests.arguments: {"command":"cd /workspaces/soiree-planner && python3 -m http.server 8000 >/tmp/soiree.log 2>&1 & sleep 1 && curl -I http://127.0.0.1:8000/index.html","explanation":"Start a simple local web server for the new app and verify that the page is served successfully.","goal":"Verify app locally","mode":"sync","timeout":20000}

arguments.command: cd /workspaces/soiree-planner && python3 -m http.server 8000 >/tmp/soiree.log 2>&1 & sleep 1 && curl -I http://127.0.0.1:8000/index.html
arguments.explanation: Start a simple local web server for the new app and verify that the page is served successfully.

data.content: The first check hit a startup hiccup, so I’m inspecting the server output directly and adjusting the launch to ensure the app is reachable.

toolRequests.name: run_in_terminal
toolRequests.arguments: {"command":"cd /workspaces/soiree-planner && python3 -m http.server 8000 --bind 127.0.0.1 > /tmp/soiree.log 2>&1 & echo $! && sleep 2 && cat /tmp/soiree.log && echo '---' && curl -I http://127.0.0.1:8000/index.html","explanation":"Retry the local server startup with explicit binding and inspect the HTTP response to confirm the page is being served.","goal":"Debug local preview","mode":"sync","timeout":20000}

arguments.command: cd /workspaces/soiree-planner && python3 -m http.server 8000 --bind 127.0.0.1 > /tmp/soiree.log 2>&1 & echo $! && sleep 2 && cat /tmp/soiree.log && echo '---' && curl -I http://127.0.0.1:8000/index.html
arguments.explanation: Retry the local server startup with explicit binding and inspect the HTTP response to confirm the page is being served.

data.content:
## Soirée Planner is ready

A simple summer-themed one-page web app is now in place with:
- a guest list showing each guest’s name, RSVP status, and plus-one
- a three-night signature drink menu
- a live countdown to golden hour on opening night
- warm dusk-inspired styling with a string-light feel

### Files
- [index.html](index.html)
- [styles.css](styles.css)
- [script.js](script.js)

### Preview
Open: http://127.0.0.1:8000/index.html

### Verification
The local preview check returned HTTP/1.0 200 OK for the page.
